passes.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This pass allows half price admission for up to four people. This e-pass is not an entry ticket. All visitors must also reserve a timed-entry ticket from the Children's Museum for admission. Visitors can reserve the museum discount e-pass through this website, then purchase a timed-entry ticket from the Children's Museum website at: https://bostonchildrensmuseum.org/ticketing . Visitors must show the date-specific museum e-pass from the Gleason Library and their timed-entry reservation to the Admissions Scanning Station at the museum. Library coupons are not valid for Sunday afternoons. Admission is $1 per person on Sundays from 1:30 -4:30 p.m.

passes.description: A winding pathway through an atrium filled with flowers, koi fish, and quail allows visitors to walk among butterflies from all over the world. Staff members are available for questions and “show and tell” demonstrations. A continuously running video explains the butterfly life cycle, and exhibits enable visitors to observe eggs, caterpillars, cocoons and chrysalides with information about each species. A gift shop is on the premises. There is a public picnic area and free parking is available.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits one person free of charge. Patrons may request one pass per week. Children under the age of three are admitted free. Seasonal schedule - 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, from Valentine’s Day through Columbus Day. You do not need to purchase tickets ahead of time or make a reservation unless you have 15 or more people. Ticket sales end at 3:30 PM, and no new customers will be admitted into the exhibits after 3:30 PM. The Butterfly Place suggests visitors arrive no later than 3:00 PM. Patrons must bring physical (paper) coupon to the Butterfly Place to receive the discount. For Museum questions, visit the Butterfly Place website https://butterflyplace-ma.com/ or call 978-392-0955

passes.description: Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson's study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on.
passes.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits up to 4 people at $5 off. For Museum questions, visit their website at www.concordmuseum.org or call 978-369-9763

passes.description: Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a “Mega Maze” in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: This pass admits up to six guests to Davis Farmland or Davis Mega Maze with a savings of $10 off the at-the-gate admission rate. Note: Adults must be accompanied by a least one child 12 years or younger when visiting Davis Farmland. Davis Farmland and Davis Mega Maze are open weather permitting. ALWAYS call or check the web before heading out. Check DavisFarmland.com or DavisMegaMaze.com for up-to-date schedules and weather-related closings. Library passes are not valid on holidays, with packages, special events or other discounts.

passes.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits up to 3 people at 1/2 price per person to the museum and Discovery Woods. Advance reservations are no longer accepted for admission. For details and questions, visit their website or call 978-264-4200.

passes.description: The EcoTarium is a unique indoor-outdoor museum in Worcester, MA. Set in an urban oasis, the museum offers a chance to walk through the treetops, take a multimedia journey through the galaxy at a digital planetarium, meet wildlife, stroll nature trails, and get hands-on with family-friendly exhibits.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: Each coupon provides a 50% discount on admission for up to 2 adults and 2 children who arrive in one vehicle. Children under two years of age are admitted free. The EcoTarium is closed on some holidays. Please check the EcoTarium website for details. Museum pass coupons do not cover the cost for tickets to the Alden Digital Planetarium, the Explorer Express Train, or special ticketed events.

passes.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
passes.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits up to 4 people at 50% off the regular admission. One member of the party must be a Massachusetts resident. May not be combined with other discounts. For Museum questions visit the HMSC website at hmsc.harvard.edu/visit or call 617-495-3045.

passes.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits up to 2 people at the discounted library price per person. Library passes are not valid for performances, events, or films. Advanced tickets are highly recommended since the museum cannot guarantee day-of reservations. Visit the museum website at https://www.icaboston.org/ or call 617-478-3100

passes.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits up to 4 people at $5 per person. Children under the age of 18 are admitted free. Visitors must reserve timed-entry tickets online at gardermuseum.org/visit or through the Box Office (617-278-5156) at least 48 hours before visiting. For Museum questions, visit their website or call 617-566-1401

passes.description: The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum, dedicated to the memory of the nation's thirty-fifth president, is located on a ten-acre park which overlooks the sea in an building designed by I.M. Pei. The Museum portrays the life, leadership, and legacy of President Kennedy, conveys his enthusiasm for politics and public service, and illustrates the nature of the office of the President, all through permanent and changing exhibits. The Library is available to students and scholars to conduct research using its collection of historical materials chronicling mid-20th century politics and the life and administration of the institution's namesake.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: Beginning on July 10, 2024, JFK Library will provide a 50% discount on admission for each visitor when they show their Gleason Public Library Card or other Massachusetts library card. No library pass is needed. Instead, each visitor should show their library card at the Admission Desk to receive the 50% discount (see details below). Visitors should present their physical or digital Massachusetts library card at the Admissions Desk to receive the 50% discount on the card holder's admission ticket. This offer is valid for visitors aged 13 and over and each visitor must present their Massachusetts library card to receive the discount. No pass is needed. Visitors aged 12 and under receive free admission. Please reach out to the JFK Library Visitor Service Department with questions at kennedy.groupvisits@nara.gov or 617-514-1600 Advanced ticket reservations are encouraged but not required. Visit https://www.jfklibrary.or/visit-museum/visit/plan-your-trip for details and to reserve tickets in advance. The last admission sale is at 3:30 p.m.

passes.description: Mass Audubon has over 55 wildlife sanctuaries and centers throughout Massachusetts where visitors can learn about and interact with nature. More than 38,000 acres of protected land deliver inspiring hilltop views to breathtaking coastal landscapes, serene woods, and working farms. Visit their website for a list of locations.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: Pass reserved at Gleason Public Library, Carlisle MA. Includes access to more than 60 wildlife sanctuaries across the state. Each pass admits up to four (4) people at $2 per person (children under 2 free) Please note that some sanctuaries may require reservations before visiting. Always check the Mass Audubon website before planning a visit: https://massaudubon.org/get-outdoors/our-wildlife-sanctuaries/trails. For questions, visit the Mass Audubon website or call 781-259-9500. Passes made available by Friends of Gleason Public Library.

passes.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
passes.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
passes.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
passes.benefits_text: Pass entitles bearer to free parking for one passenger vehicle. The Annual Parking Pass is not valid for parking at Quabbin Reservoir Boat Launch Areas, the North Point hourly rate lot in Cambridge, or any on-street Department of Conservation and Recreation location that charges an hourly rate. For questions, visit their website at https://www.mass.gov/guides/parking-at-massachusetts-state-parks or call 617-626-1420.

passes.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits two adults at $10 each for general admission and special exhibition and up to 4 children aged 7-17 at $10 each for special exhibition only. There is no discount for children 7-17 for general admission (currently $10) . Children 6 and under are admitted free. The Museum of Fine Arts suggests reserving tickets prior to your visit. Once you reserve the museum pass through this website, you will receive a confirmation email with a promo-code. Use this code to reserve tickets on the MFA website or present it at the MFA admissions desk to receive your discount when you visit the museum. For museum questions call the MFA customer service line at 1-800-440-6975.

passes.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This pass will admit up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are not included in the count of 4 and are always admitted for free. Your Library pass can only be used on the date printed on the pass. Please bring your library pass confirmation (printed or displayed on a phone) when you visit. When you arrive at the Museum, stop at the Box Office to exchange your library pass and purchase your Exhibit Hall tickets at the discounted rate. If you have any questions or would like to reserve your Exhibit Hall passes in advance to avoid waiting in line the day of your visit, please call 617-723-2500 (9 a.m. - 5 p.m. daily). If you are unable to show your library confirmation on a phone, you may print out a copy using the link in the confirmation email or request a paper copy from the library. Library: Gleason Public Library

passes.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: Library ID: 45737 This coupon allows 50% off admission to the New England Aquarium and applies to adult and child pricing. Children under the age of 3 are admitted free. This coupon is not a ticket. Visitors should redeem this coupon in the General Admission line at the Aquarium's ticket booth for discounted admission for up to 4 people. Please visit neaq.org/coupons for details and for updated hours of operation. Review the Aquarium’s website at www.neaq.org/safety ahead of your visit to ensure a safe and accessible experience. This coupon is valid for Aquarium admission only. It does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo sales. The coupon may not be sold, has no cash value, and is void if altered or copied.

passes.museum_name: New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill
passes.description: Located in Boylston, MA, and open year-round, the New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill features carefully planned gardens and collections of ornamental, edible and native plants, with a number of trails that traverse the 200-acre property. In addition, there are many programs and events, including classes, lectures, art exhibits and concerts. The Garden is run by the Worcester County Horticultural Society, the third oldest active horticultural society in the United States.
passes.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits 2 guests at half price. Online reservations are no longer required for admission to New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill, but are available for those who would like to purchase tickets in advance. Visitors to NEBG Tower Hill will need to bring the physical library pass with them to receive the discounted admission fee. If you would like to reserve tickets online, first reserve the NEBG Tower Hill pass through this website. You will receive a confirmation email which includes a promo-code. Visit the NEBG Tower Hill website at www.nebg.org and use the library promo-code at check out. You must also bring the library pass with you to enter NEBG Tower Hill at the discounted admission fee. For Museum questions, call: 508-869-6111, or visit the website at www.nebg.org

passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: Half priced admission for up to 2 adults and 4 youths. Patrons should use the Timed-Entry Admission site on the Orchard House website to secure a pre-paid reservation at the 50% discount. Please note: The discount is not applicable to special events, educational programs, large group tours, or merchandise purchases in the Museum Store. For Museum questions, see their website or call 978-369-4118

passes.description: The Peabody Essex Museum grew out of the collection of East India Marine Society, an organization of Salem captains whose charter called for acquisition of “natural and artificial curiosities.” Its permanent collection contains art and artifacts of Africa, Asia, India, China (with a Qing Dynasty house brought from the Huizhou region), Japan, Korea, Pacific Islands, and features objects of maritime art and history that include paintings, drawings, ship models, marine decorative arts, tools, weapons, navigational instruments, and ship and yacht plans. It also has a thriving changing exhibition schedule that sometimes includes important traveling shows.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits a maximum of two adult visitors at a reduced admission fee of $12 per person any day the museum is open. Children under age 16 are admitted free. Admission to special ticketed exhibitions may require a separate fee.

passes.description: Based on actual trial documents and using life-size stage sets with figures, lighting, and narration, the museum's principle exhibit examines the 1692 Salem witch trials. In a second exhibit, “Witches: Evolving Perceptions,” guides take you through changing interpretations of witches, the truth behind the stereotypes, witchcraft practice today, and witch hunting. A gift shop is on the premises.
passes.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits up to 6 people for a discounted price. Visitors must present the bright orange pass from the library to receive the discount. Paper passes or electronic passes are not accepted. Library passes are not accepted at Salem Witch Museum in the month of October. For Museum questions, call: 978-744-1692 or visit their website www.salemwitchmuseum.com

passes.description: The Trustees of the Reservations preserve and protect more than 100 special properties of exceptional scenic, historic, and ecological value—nearly 25,000 acres—all around Massachusetts. From Crane's Beach in Ipswich and World's End in Hingham to The Old Manse in Concord and Fruitlands Museum in Harvard, visitors to Trustees sites enjoy local history and nature in its various forms: beaches, meadows, forests, swamps and more.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: GO Pass coupon admits 2 adults and children under 18 to Trustees properties. Some Trustees locations require that visitors go online to reserve a timed-entry. Visit the Trustees website for up-to-date information: www.thetrustees.org/ GO Passes are valid for admission only. They are not valid for discounts at parking kiosks, stores, cafes, Inns, campgrounds, at special events or programs, on equipment rentals, or in place of any permits or passes. GO Pass admission is equivalent to a Trustees Family membership admission price at Trustees properties that charge an admission fee. Locations include: DeCordova, Fruitlands Museum; Crane Estate; the Old Manse; and many more. Admission rates vary. ***Patrons need to check the operating hours for each specific property when reserving the pass. The GO Pass covers many properties with different operating hours throughout the year*** Please note only 1 GO Pass may be reserved per person.

passes.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
passes.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: Admission to the USS Constitution Museum is on a first-come, first served basis. USS Constitution ship has limited hours. Please check website for open days. Each pass admits up to 5 people for free. For Museum questions, visit their website (ussconstitutionmuseum.org/visit) or call 617-426-1812

passes.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: Each pass admits up to 6 people with a $9.00 admission fee per adult and a $6.00 admission fee per child. Children under the age of two are admitted free. This pass can be used for daytime admission to Stone Zoo or Franklin Park Zoo. Please check the Zoo's website for hours and other questions, or call: 617-541-LION (5466).
